directives.title: Acquire Authorized Payment Token from Stripe Payment Link
steps: Receive a Stripe approved payment link URL, which is pre-configured for a specific purchase.
Initiate an HTTP GET request to the provided Stripe payment link URL.
Parse the response body or the URL itself to extract the embedded authorized payment token for the purchase.
Store the extracted payment token securely within the agent's payment handler module for subsequent transaction execution.
directives.rationale: The transcript explicitly states, 'you can point your agent at a link URL and the agent will pick up an authorized token for a purchase.' This provides a clear, actionable sequence for an autonomous agent to acquire authorization via Stripe.

directives.title: Process Google AP2 Mandate for Task Authorization
steps: Receive a Google Agent Payments Protocol (AP2) mandate (permission slip).
Parse the AP2 mandate to extract the defined scope of the task.
Parse the AP2 mandate to extract the specified constraints applicable to the task.
Parse the AP2 mandate to verify the embedded proof of user approval for the agent's action.
Store the extracted task scope, constraints, and user approval proof within the agent's authorization context for runtime enforcement and auditing.
directives.rationale: The transcript states Google AP2 'provides a mandate or permission slip that generates the scope of the task, the constraints, and the proof that the user approved the agent's action.' This implies a clear procedural requirement for an agent to consume and interpret such a mandate to proceed with authorized actions.

directives.title: Implement X42 Payment Flow for Resource Access
steps: Initiate a web request from the software to an X42-enabled service to request a specific resource.
Receive and process payment instructions provided by the service in response to the resource request.
Execute the payment as specified by the received instructions.
Upon successful payment, gain access to the requested resource.
directives.target: Software Agent / Client component interacting with X42 protocol
directives.rationale: The transcript explicitly outlines a four-step, sequential process ('software requests a resource, gets payment instructions, pays, gets access') which describes an implementable interaction flow for a software agent or system component.
